name: Missouri Central Bus
owner_or_parent: North America Central School Bus (NACSB) - owned by H.I.G. Capital (acquired Aug 2023)
principals: NACSB executive team based in Joliet, IL
districts_or_customers: Formerly St. Louis Public Schools (contract terminated July 2024)
Chicago-area districts under Illinois Central School Bus LLC d/b/a Missouri Central
recent_news: Missouri Central terminated St. Louis Public Schools contract a year early in March 2024 after a noose was discovered at the facility and ~100 drivers/mechanics protested; ~300 layoffs followed (2024). Parent NACSB acquired by H.I.G. Capital in Aug 2023.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate. This is a branded operating unit of NACSB, the #5 US school-bus operator, already owned by H.I.G. Capital. Would be a strategic-scale combination, not a regional tuck-in, and would carry recent reputational/operational baggage from the SLPS exit.

name: Columbia Bus Resources LLC
recent_news: No material public news identified. Company name in source list appears to be a misspelling of 'Columbia Business Resources LLC' at 24195 SW Boones Ferry Rd, Tualatin OR - that entity is listed as a management consultancy, not a school-bus operator. Public records do not confirm school-bus operations at this Tualatin PO Box.
fit_notes: Likely a data-error or misclassified record - the matching Tualatin entity reads as a management consultancy, not a yellow-bus contractor. Tualatin-area school transport is dominated by Mid Columbia Bus Co (Forest Grove) and First Student. Recommend de-prioritizing until operating status is independently confirmed.

name: Salter's Express Co Inc
principals: James D. 'Jim' Salter (President/owner-operator for 65+ years, deceased Aug 31, 2025, age 88)
recent_news: Founder/owner Jim Salter passed away Aug 31, 2025 after 65+ years operating the company; succession to sons Mark and/or James P. Salter not publicly confirmed (2025).
fit_notes: Classic tuck-in profile: small (~60 employees) family-owned Simsbury, CT contractor with a single anchor district (Simsbury PS) and founder just deceased (Aug 2025) - highest possible succession signal. Worth direct outreach to Mark Salter / James P. Salter. CT is a strategic Summit footprint state.

name: Winkle Bus Co
principals: Craig J. Winkle Sr. (current owner/contact, Orange CT)
Chris F. Winkle III (former President, deceased March 2018)
Christian F. Winkle IV (son of Chris III, San Diego)
districts_or_customers: Private schools: Lauralton Hall, Sacred Heart Academy, St. (others)
recent_news: Long-time President Chris F. Winkle III passed away March 18, 2018; business has continued as a fourth-generation family operation under Craig Winkle Sr. (2018 leadership transition).
fit_notes: Highly attractive CT tuck-in: 4th-generation family-owned, 200+ vehicle fleet, multi-city anchor contracts (Milford, West Haven, North Haven) plus private-school book - much larger than the 60-employee tag suggests (employee count likely reflects one location only). Post-2018 generational transition supports succession dialogue with Craig Winkle Sr.

name: A School Ride LLC
principals: Founders are BASIS Scottsdale parents (names not publicly disclosed)
districts_or_customers: Planned expansion: BASIS Phoenix, BASIS Phoenix Primary, Scottsdale Prep, Archway Classical Academy-Scottsdale
recent_news: No material M&A news; company has steadily grown from 1 minivan/7 students in 2009 to ~300 students across 5 routes serving BASIS charter schools and elite private schools in Scottsdale/Phoenix.
fit_notes: Niche private-pay shuttle operator (parent-billed at ~$2,350/student/year) serving charter/private schools - NOT a traditional district-contract school-bus business. Small fleet (~5 shuttles of 28-35 pax). Limited fit for Summit district-contract platform, though could be an entry point into AZ charter-school transport. Employee count in source data (53) appears overstated for the operation described on the website.
